message:
chore(ft-xbnl0.2.3): silence dead-code warnings on unused cx-first retry helpers (tick 305)

Adds `#[allow(dead_code)]` to three cx-first retry helpers in
`WeztermClient` that exist as speculative scaffolding but are not
currently wired into any production code path:

- `run_cli_with_pane_check_retry_with_cx` (wezterm.rs L2353)
- `run_cli_with_retry_with_cx` (L2379)
- `retry_with_with_cx` (L2423)

These are the cx-first siblings of the ambient retry helpers. The
ambient versions are still in use (from the legacy `wait_for_with_cx`
path etc); the cx-first versions await a future tick that wires the
CLI invocations through them.

Silencing the dead-code warnings removes the "never used" noise from
the baseline so future regressions show up more clearly. Reduces
baseline warning count 5 → 4.

Cumulative ft-xbnl0.2.3 ticks this session: 119 (187-305).
frankenterm-core internal sites: 68 (unchanged; #[allow(dead_code)]
  doesn't count as a migration site).
main.rs cross-crate total: 236 sites (unchanged).

Grand total: 304 cx-first migrations (unchanged — this tick is a
warning-silencing cleanup, not a new migration).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
